title: Seeing God Face to Face | Bhagavad Gita Chapter 11 Verses 45 - 49 | HG Amitasana Das | HKM Mumbai
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: So today we are covering text number 45 to 49 from the 11th chapter of the Gita and the title for today's discussion is Seeing God Face to Face.
By the way, how many of us have seen God? Others? Yet to see? Hoping to see? Okay.
Prabhupada was asked, have you seen God? And depending on the audience, whether they were devotees or they were completely raw, he at different times gave different answer.
Not that because he hasn't seen God, but depending on the audience, he gave different answer. I'll share that as we move forward in this discussion today.
So, based on the teachings of Srila Prabhupada, his wonderful insights in the purports, let's chant this verse together.
Adrishta purvam rishito asmi drishtva, bhayena cha pravitham mano me, da deva me darshaya deva roopam, prasida devesha jagan nivasa.
After seeing this universal form, which I have never seen before,
I am gladdened, but at the same time, my mind is disturbed with fear.
Therefore, please bestow your grace upon me and reveal again your form as the personality of Godhead, O Lord of Lords, O Abode of the Universe.
These are the words spoken by Arjuna to Krishna after having seen the universal form.
So, here he said, I am gladdened.
Can anyone help me with an answer?
Why Arjuna is glad that he saw the universal form?
And apparently there is a contradiction.
Simultaneously he is saying, I am fearful.
On one side he is saying, I am glad.
The other side, he is also mentioning that he is fearful.
So, he is glad that the Lord is so opulent.
So, he is glad that the Lord is so opulent.
All the energies, all the potencies, all the devas are within him.
He is the source of everything.
So, that made him glad that Krishna is not an ordinary person.
That person who is with him, who is his companion, who is his sarathi in the battle, accompanying him, with him, is not an ordinary personality.
It is the Supreme Lord Himself.
So, that is the source of his happiness.
He is fearful because till now he never had the understanding that Krishna whom he was treating like a friend, like an ordinary driver, was not an ordinary personality.
And in the previous verse we discussed, he said, unknowingly, unintentionally, I might have committed many, many mistakes.
He might have committed many, many offenses by calling him by casual names.
Sometimes, you know, when we don't realize somebody's greatness, maybe we may go to an office and we don't know who the boss is.
And we may find somebody walking around and, you know, may not respect him.
But later on when we recognize that he is the boss, maybe we have mixed feelings.
Some element of, you know, I shouldn't have acted like this.
Some element of, you know, I shouldn't have acted like this.
So, same is the case with Arjuna here.
Now, the universal form which Arjuna saw, it's not eternal form of the Lord.
It is material form and it is temporary.
It was shown to Krishna, so Arjuna, and it was wound up.
Now, Arjuna is requesting Krishna, yes, I have seen your universal form.
Now, I am keen on seeing your original form, the spiritual form, the four-handed form.
The next verse is about Arjuna requesting for that.
So, the original form of the Lord, this brings us to the point that many people believe that Lord has no form.
God is some kind of a power, some kind of energy.
But the understanding we get from the scriptures that God, no doubt, has innumerable energies.
But behind that energy is the energetic.
There is universal intelligence which is running the whole cosmos.
But behind that intelligence is the intelligent creator.
Whose intelligence? Whose energy? From where it is emanating?
So, just like you and me are a person, God also is a person.
But He is not an ordinary person.
Generally, why do we feel difficult to accept God as a person?
we have come across people, individuals, beings, who are limited in time and space.
Suppose today, you are here in the class.
I can very confidently say that you are in Mumbai.
If somebody comes and says, no, so and so is out of station.
No, I saw him in the class.
Because, it is very, very obvious that if you are here in the class,
you are not out of station.
On the other side, we know that Lord is omnipresent.
So, if we give a form to the Lord,
the Lord has a form, He is a person,
then that quality of the Lord that He is omnipresent,
He is present everywhere, He is present in each and every atom,
He is present in everything, becomes difficult to comprehend.
So, to safely reconcile with this understanding,
people conclude that God is not a person.
It's an energy. It's in power.
why we come to the conclusion that God is not a person, because
we know that person has a body,
which grows, stays for some time, reproduces,
And we know that Lord is
He is beyond these six transformations.
Hence, we conclude that since our body goes through transformation,
we go through diseases, our body becomes old,
we die, but God doesn't do that.
He doesn't die, He doesn't become old, He doesn't become diseased.
So, it is safe to conclude that He doesn't have a form.
But our scriptures point out that the Lord has a form.
But His form is Sat Chit Ananda Vigraha.
It is not an ordinary form.
It is not a material form.
And what is the quality or nature of that form?
It is Sat. It is eternal.
That's why it is important that when we come to
it is difficult to come to right conclusion
sheer by logic and rational thinking.
What I mentioned right now, logically it appears right.
He is omnipresent, therefore He doesn't have a form.
But we cannot comprehend the Lord or come to understand about the Lord
merely by our logical conclusion or analytical skills.
We have to understand about the Lord from Lord Himself and His pure devotees.
So here Arjuna is saying, please show me that form,
the Lord in innumerable spiritual planets which are also called Vaikuntalokas.
He has spiritual form, transcendental form with Shankha, Chakra,
O Universal Lord, I wish to see you in your four-armed form,
with helmeted head and with club, wheel, conch and lotus flower in your hands.
I long to see you in that form.
Now many times when we have some picture of the Lord,
so sometimes people say, is it some kind of imagination?
How does the Lord actually look like?
So in the scriptures, there are vivid descriptions about the form of the Lord.
Like here you said, with helmet, head, with club, wheel, conch and lotus flower in your hands.
So we get some elements of what the form has.
What is in the hands, what is on the head.
So like that, the vivid descriptions about the Lord in the spiritual world
and basis which Acharyas have written commentaries,
given description about the Lord, glorified the Lord.
King Kuleshekha Alwar, he has Mukundamala Shruta, he is describing the glories of the Lord.
How does he look like? How his hairs are?
How his facial expressions are? How his eyes are?
And that's how we understand about the Lord.
Now here we have the Lord replying.
The blessed Lord said, My dear Arjuna, happily do I show you this universal form within this material world by my internal potency.
No one before you has ever seen this unlimited and glaringly effulgent form.
Krishna gave him that divine vision.
And before that, no one had seen that universal form.
As I said, it's not an eternal form.
No one had seen this universal form of Lord before Arjuna.
Now just like Arjuna was blessed with the divine vision,
even when we are blessed with divine vision by the Lord,
the Lord revealed himself to us.
That is the process of seeing God.
Prabhupada in one place mentions when Prabhupada was asked,
He says, Do not be eager to see God.
Act in a way God sees you.
In other words, Lord has given innumerable instructions for us.
And if we follow that instructions,
the Lord will be pleased with us.
And then we can experience the love, the reciprocation of the Lord.
Otherwise, in one sense, we are disqualified.
In the battlefield, Arjuna was also there.
Arjuna recognized Krishna as the supreme personality of God that Duryodhana could not recognize.
no one before you has ever seen this universal form of mine.

by being a very learned scholar,

so when I first started going to the Iskand temple
so first I met the devotees
the chairman whom we were interacting with those days
he was from IIT Mumbai alumni

so I was talking to them
and you know they are science graduates

and I used to appreciate prasadam a lot

can anyone guess what is that
It took lot of time. It was people, devotees dancing, jumping and dancing wildly in Kirtans.
First time when I saw the devotees, I was taken aback. What is happening? Is this also Bhakti?
Jumping, hitting the drums and jumping.
Because typically when you see some spiritualist in a church, walking slowly, grave.
So what kind of devotion is this? This is a big question mark.
And specially if you go in a Kirtan in the temple, when the Kirtan is on and if you are standing like this, they will pull you in.
So whenever Kirtan time was in, I used to just walk out of the temple.
And Prasadam time I would enter back.
So this went on for many days.
Because Kirtan means jumping like this and everything was fine but why they are jumping was very big question mark for me.
How many of us have seen ISKCON, Kirtan, people jumping and dancing? Anyone?
Most of you have seen, yeah.
So one day, so I used to like hearing Bhagavad Gita class.
Would like talking to the devotees.
And would also obviously like Prasadam.
But the jumping and dancing part was alien to me.
So one day I was hearing in a class, the speaker told, you know two kinds of people cannot dance.
So, you know my eyes, ears, what is he saying, he is talking about me.
Two kinds of people cannot dance.
Today we will dance, let us see who can't dance.
Two kinds of people cannot dance.
So I said, you know, I was curious to know what are these two kinds of people.
He says one, those who are envious of God.
Those who are not in God's party.
Again it was difficult, how can he say that I am envious of God and therefore I am not dancing?
How can I be, otherwise why will I come to the temple?
So then I thought, maybe it is just one way of enthusing me to dance.
It appeared to me that whole conversation was directed, keeping me in mind.
One thing is, who is envious of the Lord, a person who is not part of God's party.
And then, I raised my hand.
I said, how do you say that, God's party and not part of, envious of God.
So he said, just like when these elections happen, you know you have political rallies.
You will have a political rally, you know, congressman, Rajiv Gandhi Jindabad, Rajiv Gandhi Jindabad.
You know BJP people, will they say Rajiv Gandhi Jindabad?
They will feel, why they are chanting Rajiv Gandhi?
When Modi will come, they will say Modi Jindabad.
You cannot chant the names of person who is of the opposing party.
Yes or no? Agreed what I am saying?
If says Jai Shri Ram, Jai Shri Ram, who will say?
Hanuman will say Jai Shri Ram.
He will not say, he cannot say.
Because he wants Sita, he doesn't want Ram.
So then he said, just like when it is name of Rama, Hanuman will chant Jai Shri Ram.
Ravana is in the opposing party.
When the battle happened, Hanuman was on the side of Ram.
Ravana was in the opposing party.
So if you are part of the opposing party, you will not chant the names of Lord.
Second, he says those who are loaded with lot of sins, burdened with sins, the sins are so overwhelming.
The burden of sin is, imagine if you have figuratively, if you are holding 50 kgs of bag in your left hand, right hand and I ask you to jump.
50 kgs on the left hand, 50 kgs on the right hand, jump.
So if we are loaded with lot of sinful reactions, we are very sinful, we are very impious, we are not pure, impurities are weighing on us, we will not be able to jump.
So after having heard that, I felt internally offended.
How can he say that I am impure, I am chanting so much, I come here.
So one day I went, the Kirtan was going on.
And you know, by the side of the wall, I was also shy.
You know, what is dancing in front of everyone?
It was not a, what will people think, you are shy.
So I went, the Kirtan was going on.
And by the side of the wall and slowly I raised my hands.
Almost till this point I raised the hand.
At least I am able to raise the hands.
That means some element of piety is there.
And then the beats of the Kirtan was so, you know, inclusive that I saw that, you know, slowly the Kirtan is going on.
And I was very conscious, anybody is not looking at me.
He will say, you know, he has started dancing.
Because I was very, very known at that time, that Kirtan time, I am away from Kirtans.
And then slowly from that day onwards,
even before I could realize, I saw that my legs are in the air and I have started little bit jumping and dancing.
So when we do Kirtans, when we dance, when we clap,
what happens is, all the sinful reactions which are there,
just like in a jungle when a lion enters, what happens?
All the animals flee out of fear.
So likewise, when the holy name of the Lord is uttered,
all the sinful reactions which are overpowering us,
over many, many lifetimes we have completed,
those sinful reactions, those sinful tendencies start fleeing away.
They start running out of fear.
Fear personified is fearful of Lord Shri Krishna.
Fear personified is fearful of Lord Shri Krishna.
So all the sinful reactions get, so to say, wiped out.
So now, part of the purification we talked about,
if we have to perceive the Lord,
the Lord who is divine, we should have divine vision.
We cannot have divine vision unless we become?
To become divine, we have to be pure internally and externally.
we have to follow the process which Acharyas have given for our internal purification.
For our internal purification and external purification also,
we talked about the four do's.
A, B, C, D. Simple formula.
Keep doing this and purification will happen.
Depending on how sincerely, how quickly and how effectively we do this,
we will have the purification happening.
Sometimes, inadvertently, unknowingly, we end up missing lot of sessions.
One day, one session, guests came to our house.
Second session, guests came to our house.
Second session, I was out of station.
Third session, you know, match was going on.
So this, always remember, we can either go in a positive loop or a negative loop.
Negative loop means if we are getting in a negative, no association, no reading, no chanting,
it will attract more of that.
The motivation to chant, motivation to read, motivation to come will reduce, will wane.
And we can completely get, so to say, entangled.

Try your best not to let big gaps come in.
Slowly what will happen is, just like imagine if we don't take bath daily, what will happen?
Just like we don't charge our batteries, it will get completely drained.
As far as possible, on a regular basis we should have.

On a daily basis we can have some association.

This is a test for all of you.
If we have to plant a tree, a sapling and get fruits,
what are the important elements which we have to take care of?
What are the important elements for that plant to grow, become a tree and have fruits?
Anyone will help me with an answer?
So I will go one by one.
First, the farmer should be right.
Just intent of growing a plant is not enough.
I got some plants for my room when I was staying in Bangalore.
And I was really geared up that I will take good care of the plants.
And every day I used to water.
And then slowly, I said, what happened to them?
Sometimes plants wilt because you forget to water.
And I went to the gardener and said,
So farmer should have the right knowledge.
What is the right season, right time, right conditions?
So what is farmer equivalent to?
What is a farmer equivalent to?
What are the other conditions required for the plant to grow?
No, you can't put a plant, a good farmer puts a seed here.
Next week we'll come, you know, we'll have a good sapling coming out of the stage.
So, the soil should be fertile.
A sincere and a good disciple.
Prabhupada says, who is a good disciple who is ready to be disciplined by the Guru?
Guru. Disciple word comes from discipline.
The do's and don'ts, he will meticulously follow the do's and don'ts.
Disciple is not an ornamental thing.
Yes, I am a disciple, he is my Guru.
So, fertile soil is equivalent to a good disciple who follows the rules and regulations,
A good farmer, fertile soil and a potent seed.
You may have a good farmer, you may have a fertile soil, but the seed is bad.
So, if these three things are there, you have a good farmer who has a knowledge of farming,
you have a fertile soil and you have a potent seed,
which is message in its pure form, coming in disciplic succession.
A genuine Guru, which is a must.
If we have a good disciple, we are ready to be disciplined by a genuine Guru,
willing to follow his instructions, prescriptions, and we have pure message.

and then we have the plant growing, blossoming, flowering, and finally having the fruits.

So, while they are working, thinking of that.
So, when you love a person, the thinking becomes natural.
So, when we develop love of God, thinking of God, remembering God, obeying Lord, following His instruction,
becomes a default mode of function.
And that is the ultimate objective.
Any questions, anything you would like to ask, and then we move on?
Why he has a divine vision?
Because he is a devotee of the Lord.
Bhakto sime sakha cheti rahasyad hi etat uttamam.
I am speaking this knowledge to you because,
bhakto sime sakha cheti, you are my bhakta, you are my devotee, you are non-envious of me.
Deep within, envy doesn't mean that we decry the Lord openly.
Envy means we want to be the centre, we want to be the enjoyer.
What is Lord? Lord means he is the enjoyer, he is the controller, he is the master, he is the proprietor.
He is the well-wisher of everyone.
When we want to take the position of the Lord, may not be very explicitly, implicitly in our actions.
We want to be the centre of attraction.
We want people to praise me.
There is no dependence on the Lord.
That tendency, lust, what is lust?
Completely out of, Lord is out of picture.
So Arjuna does not have that.
Bhakto seme sakha cheti rahasyadhi eta uttamam.
Now I am going to share this rahasya because you are a bhakta.
Krishna also says in the Gita,
Naham prakasha sarvasya yogo maya samanvrita.
I do not reveal myself to everyone.
And I am concealed by my yoga maya potency.
So when we become devotees of the Lord, the Lord reveals himself.
